zh.lifeSummary: 當William Josiah Wolcott於1610年出生在英國時，他的父親Edward Wolcott41歲，母親Mary Phippen40歲。他於大約1630年與Alice Ingersoll在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1660年在美國麻薩諸塞州艾可薩萊姆享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wolcott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Surrey): habitational name from Wollacott in Thrushelton (Devon), named from the Old English personal name Wulfnōth + cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’. This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

History: Henry Wolcott (1578–1655), clothier, came from Tolland, Somerset, England, and settled in Windsor, CT, in 1636. His grandson Roger (1679–1767) was colonial governor of CT; his great-grandson Oliver (1726–1797) was a signer of the Declaration of Independence.

variants.name: Woolcock
meanings: English (Cornwall): probably a variant of   Wolcott  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Josiah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Meaning ‘God heals’ in Hebrew; it is borne by a king of Judah, whose story is recounted in 2 Kings 22–23. This was fairly frequently used as a given name in the English-speaking world, especially among Dissenters, from the 18th to the late 19th century. Following several decades of disuse, this name has become increasingly fashionable since the 1970s, especially in the U.S. The spelling Josias was also in use among Puritans from the 16th century. The most famous English bearer is the potter Josiah Wedgwood ( 1730–95 ). In North America this was a recurrent name in the Quincy family of Massachusetts; the best-known Josiah Quincy ( 1744–75 ) was a pre-Revolutionary patriot, who died while returning from arguing the cause of the American colonists in London.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Josiah Wolcott was born in 1610, in England, his father, Edward Wolcott, was 41 and his mother, Mary Phippen, was 40. He married Alice Ingersoll about 1630, in Salem, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1660, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 50.

ko.Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘
ko.Salem, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 살렘
ko.lifeSummary: William Josiah Wolcott(이)가 1610년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Wolcott(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Phippen(은)는 40세였다. 그는 대략 1630년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 살렘에서 Alice Ingersoll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1660년 미국 매사추세츠 주 에세스 살렘에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Josiah Wolcott родился в 1610 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Wolcott было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Phippen, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Ingersoll около 1630 г. в местности сеилем, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1660 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 50 лет.
